evidence.title: Official MCP Registry live sample
evidence.summary: The official registry returned 100 records in the observed page, including 30 latest-version entries and 25 remotely callable servers.
caveats: One registry page measures supply, not paid demand.
A listed remote does not prove reliability or safety.

evidence.title: Official MCP Inspector
evidence.summary: The official open-source Inspector already provides interactive UI and CLI testing for tools, resources, prompts, local servers, and remote transports.
caveats: The paid offer must sell a repeatable decision outcome and repair work, not duplicate free protocol debugging.

evidence.title: x402 Bazaar utility endpoint sample
evidence.summary: The 30-resource sample reported 18,284 calls and a sum of 10,718 resource-level unique-payer counters over 30 days; leading entries were concrete email and blockchain utilities.
caveats: Resource-level unique-payer counters may include the same payer across multiple resources and must not be described as globally unique buyers.
Marketplace utility demand does not prove demand for this exact preflight offer.

evidence.title: Public MCP help-wanted issue scan
evidence.summary: The public search returned 491 matching open issues; none of the 25-item sample contained an explicit dollar bounty.
caveats: Issue volume indicates implementation friction, not purchase intent.

opportunities.name: MCP / Agent Preflight Full Audit
opportunities.thesis: A free deterministic preflight can qualify demand and prove the engine, while a fixed audit sells an authorized live protocol test, prioritized decision report, and one repair rather than competing with the free MCP Inspector.
opportunities.offer: Within 48 hours, run public-metadata checks plus authorized live MCP or agent protocol tests, deliver a prioritized connection-readiness report, and implement one agreed repair patch.
opportunities.intended_buyer: Agent builders and small teams deciding whether an MCP server or agent endpoint is ready to connect or launch.
opportunities.acquisition_channel: Functional GitHub Pages preflight, public GitHub intake, and a human service marketplace only after owner activation.
opportunities.payment_rail: Public human checkout or Contra for the full audit; checkout URL remains pending.
opportunities.expected_outcome: One verified $149 audit customer or three qualified requests for authorized live preflight work.
human_actions_required: Complete identity, tax, bank, and payout setup for the selected human checkout rail.
Grant target access only when a paid audit requires private code or credentials.
free_substitutes: Open-source schema and protocol test scripts
opportunities.next_executable_action: Run the functional public demo and collect qualified full-audit requests through the connected GitHub intake.
required_human_setup: Human checkout identity and payout verification only before accepting payment
validation_72h: At least 3 external repositories evaluated by named testers or qualified issue submitters
At least 1 request for an authorized live protocol test or repair
No checkout infrastructure expansion without qualified intent

kill_criteria.reason: Kill the full-audit offer after 25 qualified exposures produce no requests.

pivot_criteria.reason: Pivot the paid scope when qualified requests do not begin checkout.

scale_criteria.reason: Scale after three verified audits and $300 net revenue.

opportunities.name: MCP / Agent Preflight Metered API
opportunities.thesis: The x402 sample shows repeated payment for concrete machine utilities, so the same preflight engine can test a low-priced agent route without assuming that generic reasoning itself has demand.
opportunities.offer: A machine-readable public-metadata preflight for free, followed by a $0.25 basic or $1 expanded public-evidence report per call after a receiving wallet and runtime host are connected; neither machine tier executes target code.
opportunities.intended_buyer: Autonomous agents and orchestrators screening MCP servers, repositories, and paid tools before connection.
opportunities.acquisition_channel: Functional public demo, machine-readable OpenAPI contract, x402 Bazaar only after successful settlement, and agent ecosystem discovery.
opportunities.payment_rail: x402 Base USDC after a dedicated receiving wallet and deployed route; no paid endpoint is active now.
opportunities.expected_outcome: Three qualified recurring-use requests, followed by the first independently verified paid API call.
human_actions_required: Provide a dedicated public receiving address only after the validation threshold.
Approve the deployed runtime host before enabling the paid route.
opportunities.next_executable_action: Deploy the free public preflight and require three qualified recurring-use signals before wallet activation.
required_human_setup: Dedicated receiving wallet and runtime host only after three qualified recurring-use signals
validation_72h: At least 1 request identifies a repeated agent workflow
No wallet claim before the threshold

kill_criteria.reason: Kill the metered offer when 100 measured qualified exposures produce no recurring-use request.

pivot_criteria.reason: Pivot report fields when qualified users request different recurring outcomes.

scale_criteria.reason: Scale after 100 independently verified paid calls.
